name: South Dakota Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in South Dakota — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

provider.name: South Dakota State Board of Dentistry

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

mainEntity.name: Does South Dakota require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, South Dakota does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in South Dakota?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in South Dakota.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in South Dakota?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in South Dakota?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in South Dakota. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.
